issue_summary: Introduce new system property for timezones
javadoc_before:
/** * Gets the default time zone. *

* The default time zone is derived from the system property {@code user.timezone}. * If that is {@code null} or is not a valid identifier, then the value of the * JDK {@code TimeZone} default is converted. If that fails, {@code UTC} is used. *

* NOTE: If the {@code java.util.TimeZone} default is updated after calling this * method, then the change will not be picked up here. * * @return the default datetime zone object */
javadoc_after:
/** * Gets the default time zone. *

* The default time zone is derived from the system property {@code org.joda.time.DateTimeZone.Timezone}. * If that is {@code null} or is not a valid identifier, then the value of the * JDK {@code TimeZone} default is converted. If that fails, {@code UTC} is used. *

* NOTE: If the {@code java.util.TimeZone} default is updated after calling this * method, then the change will not be picked up here. *

* NOTE: This previously checked the {@code user.timezone} property, see issue #587. * * @return the default datetime zone object */
